requirements.title: Scenario Path Selector
requirements.description: Provide a visible scenario button for unclear requirements / idea exploration.
requirements.acceptance: Selecting the scenario highlights the recommended path and surfaces `maestro-brainstorm` as the next command.

requirements.title: Interactive Workflow Map
requirements.description: Show Maestro flow nodes as clickable and keyboard-accessible map elements.
requirements.acceptance: Users can select nodes and see the selected node reflected in the details panel.

requirements.title: Path Highlighting
requirements.description: Highlight the route for unclear requirements from intent input through brainstorm-oriented routing.
requirements.acceptance: The highlighted nodes and edges visually differ from inactive paths.

requirements.title: Five-Part Node Explanation
requirements.description: Each relevant node explains what it does, input, output, next step, and common mistake.
requirements.acceptance: Every highlighted node contains all five explanation fields.

requirements.title: Stepwise Next-Command Guide
requirements.description: After each highlighted command, show the output condition and the next command choices available from that state.
requirements.acceptance: Users can follow a scenario from first command through subsequent command choices until the stated goal is satisfied.

requirements.title: Internal Validation Prompt
requirements.description: Capture whether internal users can choose the command sequence after using the map.
requirements.acceptance: The page includes a simple feedback or checklist prompt for `I can choose the next command at each step until the goal is met`.

requirements.title: Future Recommendation Placeholder
requirements.description: Reserve space for future rule-based or agent-backed command recommendation.
requirements.acceptance: UI does not imply free-form recommendation is available in MVP.

constraints.constraint: The site must remain independent from `maestro-flow/docs-site`.
constraints.rationale: The original docs site targets project documentation, while this product validates interactive learning and command selection.

constraints.constraint: The MVP must prioritize scenario-based path highlighting and stepwise next-command selection for unclear requirements.
constraints.rationale: Internal validation focuses on whether users can choose the first command and then continue choosing follow-up commands until the scenario goal is satisfied.

domain.problem_statement: Users face command selection uncertainty when Maestro requirements are still unclear.
domain.audience: Internal users validating the workflow wiki concept.

terminology.definition: Clickable and keyboard-accessible visual node that exposes input, output, responsibility, and next action.
Explicit scenario button that highlights the relevant workflow path without natural-language parsing.

non_goals.title: Reuse maestro-flow docs-site
non_goals.rationale: Product positioning differs from the upstream documentation site.

non_goals.title: LLM-backed recommendation in MVP
non_goals.rationale: Scenario buttons are enough for the first internal validation.

insights.summary: The first version should validate whether an interactive path map teaches internal users to choose a command sequence for unclear requirements, from first command through follow-up commands until the goal is met.

open_questions.question: Should later versions add free-form command recommendation through rules or an LLM-backed agent?
